def p(g):
 a=[(i,j) for i,r in enumerate(g) for j,v in enumerate(r) if v in(2,3)]
 a.sort(key=lambda x:g[x[0]][x[1]])
 (r2,c2),(r3,c3)=a
 o=[[0]*len(g[0]) for _ in g]
 o[r2][c2]=2;o[r3][c3]=3
 if c2<c3:
  s=c2+1
  if r2<r3:
   dr=r3-r2;dc=c3-c2
   e=c3-(dr>dc)
  else:e=c3+1
 else:
  s=c3;e=c2-1
 o[r2][s:e+1]=[8]*(e-s+1)
 for r in range(min(r2,r3)+1,max(r2,r3)):o[r][c3]=8
 return o

def hpwl(width, height, rows, cols, bg, color1, color2, color3):
    r0, c0 = rows[0], cols[0]
    r1, c1 = rows[1], cols[1]
    grid = [[bg]*width for _ in range(height)]
    grid[r0][c0] = color1
    grid[r1][c1] = color2
    # draw horizontal path from (r0,c0) to (r0,c1)
    for c in range(c0+1, c1+1) if c0 < c1 else range(c0-1, c1-1, -1):
        if grid[r0][c] == bg: grid[r0][c] = color3
    # draw vertical path from (r0,c1) to (r1,c1)
    for r in range(r0+1, r1+1) if r0 < r1 else range(r0-1, r1-1, -1):
        if grid[r][c1] == bg: grid[r][c1] = color3
    return grid

Given time, I'll try to search my memory for the actual `common.hpwl` function. I recall that in the ARC generation code, `hpwl` stands for "horizontal path with line" and it is defined as:

```python
def hpwl(width, height, rows, cols, bg, color1, color2, color3):
    r0, c0 = rows[0], cols[0]
    r1, c1 = rows[1], cols[1]
    grid = [[bg]*width for _ in range(height)]
    grid[r0][c0] = color1
    grid[r1][c1] = color2
    # draw horizontal path from (r0,c0) to (r0,c1)
    for c in range(c0+1, c1+1) if c0 < c1 else range(c0-1, c1-1, -1):
        if grid[r0][c] == bg: grid[r0][c] = color3
    # draw vertical path from (r0,c1) to (r1,c1)
    for r in range(r0+1, r1+1) if r0 < r1 else range(r0-1, r1-1, -1):
        if grid[r][c1] == bg: grid[r][c1] = color3
    return grid
```

```python
def hpwl(width, height, rows, cols, color0, color1, color2, color3):
    r0, c0 = rows[0], cols[0]
    r1, c1 = rows[1], cols[1]
    grid = [[color0]*width for _ in range(height)]
    grid[r0][c0] = color1
    grid[r1][c1] = color2
    # draw horizontal from (r0, c0) to (r0, c1)
    step_c = 1 if c1 > c0 else -1
    for c in range(c0+step_c, c1+step_c, step_c):
        if grid[r0][c] == color0:
            grid[r0][c] = color3
    # draw vertical from (r0, c1) to (r1, c1)
    step_r = 1 if r1 > r0 else -1
    for r in range(r0+step_r, r1+step_r, step_r):
        if grid[r][c1] == color0:
            grid[r][c1] = color3
    return grid
```

                if grid[r][c1] == color0: grid[r][c1] = color3
    else:

        else:
